criteria.label: Technical capability and solution quality
criteria.scoring_guidance: Score 0-10. Assess: technical architecture quality, proposed technology stack suitability, scalability and performance provisions, security-by-design approach, alignment with NSW Government technology standards. 10 = exemplary solution exceeding all requirements; 5 = meets requirements with some gaps; 0 = fails to address requirements.

criteria.label: Implementation approach and project management
criteria.scoring_guidance: Score 0-10. Assess: project methodology (preference for agile/iterative), delivery roadmap realism, risk identification and mitigation, change management approach, transition and go-live planning. 10 = highly credible, detailed and risk-aware plan; 5 = adequate plan with gaps; 0 = no credible plan.

criteria.label: Team capability and relevant experience
criteria.scoring_guidance: Score 0-10. Assess: key personnel CVs and relevant experience, past delivery of similar NSW Government or equivalent projects, team stability and key-person risk, certifications. 10 = highly experienced team with directly relevant reference projects; 5 = adequate experience with some gaps; 0 = insufficient experience.

criteria.label: Value for money — price and cost structure
criteria.scoring_guidance: Score 0-10. Assess: total cost of ownership (not just upfront cost), pricing transparency, rate schedules, ongoing support cost structure, value of optional items included. Scoring relative to other tenderers — highest value score goes to best price-quality combination. 10 = best value; 0 = poorest value or pricing not credible.

criteria.label: Risk management and resilience
criteria.scoring_guidance: Score 0-10. Assess: identification of delivery risks, cyber security provisions, business continuity and disaster recovery, insurance coverage, exit and transition obligations. 10 = comprehensive risk management exceeding requirements; 5 = adequate provisions; 0 = inadequate risk awareness.

criteria.label: AI solution design and technical quality
criteria.scoring_guidance: Score 0-10. Assess: AI/ML model design, training data approach, model accuracy and validation methodology, explainability provisions, alignment with NSW AIAF requirements. 10 = exemplary design with strong explainability; 5 = adequate but gaps in explainability or validation; 0 = fails AIAF requirements.

criteria.label: Human oversight and governance provisions
criteria.scoring_guidance: Score 0-10. Assess: human-in-the-loop design, override mechanisms, audit logging of AI decisions, bias monitoring provisions, AIAF compliance documentation. A minimum score of 6/10 is required — failure to provide adequate human oversight provisions is a mandatory disqualifier.

criteria.label: Data governance and privacy
criteria.scoring_guidance: Score 0-10. Assess: training data documentation (sources, quality, bias testing), data sovereignty provisions, privacy-by-design approach, data retention and deletion policies. 10 = exemplary data governance; 5 = adequate but gaps; 0 = fails privacy requirements.

criteria.label: Implementation approach and team AI expertise
criteria.scoring_guidance: Score 0-10. Assess: team AI/ML credentials and track record, deployment methodology, change management for AI adoption, training for agency staff. 10 = highly experienced AI team with government delivery track record; 5 = adequate; 0 = insufficient AI expertise.

criteria.label: Value for money
criteria.scoring_guidance: Score 0-10. Assess: total cost relative to AI capability delivered, pricing transparency, ongoing model maintenance costs, exit costs. Relative scoring against other tenderers.

criteria.label: Risk and security
criteria.scoring_guidance: Score 0-10. Assess: adversarial attack protections, model drift monitoring, cyber security provisions for AI system, incident response for model failures.

criteria.label: Relevant expertise and methodology
criteria.scoring_guidance: Score 0-10. Assess: proposed approach and methodology, domain expertise, understanding of the problem, innovation in approach. 10 = highly tailored, well-reasoned approach; 5 = generic but adequate; 0 = does not address the brief.

criteria.label: Team capability and past experience
criteria.scoring_guidance: Score 0-10. Assess: key personnel experience, equivalent past engagements, client references, team composition. 10 = highly experienced team with directly comparable engagements; 5 = adequate; 0 = insufficient.

criteria.label: Value for money
criteria.scoring_guidance: Score 0-10. Assess: fee structure against scope, day rates vs market, deliverable clarity. Relative scoring.

criteria.label: Risk and probity
criteria.scoring_guidance: Score 0-10. Assess: conflicts of interest declaration, independence provisions, insurance coverage, sub-contracting arrangements.

criteria.label: Product quality and specification compliance
criteria.scoring_guidance: Score 0-10. Assess: compliance with technical specification, quality certifications, product samples or evidence, warranty provisions. 10 = fully compliant with exceeding quality; 0 = non-compliant.

criteria.label: Price and total cost
criteria.scoring_guidance: Score 0-10. Assess: unit prices, delivery costs, payment terms, volume discounts. Relative scoring against other tenderers.

criteria.label: Delivery and logistics
criteria.scoring_guidance: Score 0-10. Assess: lead times, delivery reliability, logistics arrangements, flexibility for volume changes.

criteria.label: Sustainability and social value
criteria.scoring_guidance: Score 0-10. Assess: environmental certifications, recycled/sustainable materials, local manufacturing, modern slavery compliance, SME or Aboriginal business participation.
